heading: Revolving fund; loans; regulations
label: 5133 Revolving fund; loans; regulations
text: The Secretary of the Interior, or his designated representative, is authorized, under such regulations as the Secretary may prescribe, to make loans from the revolving fund established pursuant to the Acts of () [ et seq.], and (), to tribes, bands, groups, and individual Indians, not otherwise eligible for loans under said Acts: , That no portion of these funds shall be loaned to Indians of less than one-quarter Indian blood.June 18, 1934June 26, 193648 Stat. 98425 U.S.C. 510149 Stat. 1967Provided
notes: References in TextAct of , referred to in text, is , , popularly known as the Indian Reorganization Act, which is classified generally to this chapter. Provisions of the Act establishing the revolving fund are set out in . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.
